steps.title: Distribute to both terms
steps.content: Multiply $8$ by each term inside the parentheses. The variable term becomes $8x^2$, and the constant term becomes $8(7)$.

steps.title: Simplify the expression
steps.content:
Compute the constant term, then combine it with the variable term:
$$8(7)=56$$
$$8(x^2+7)=8x^2+56$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A has the wrong constant, $1$. Choice B leaves $7$ undistributed. Choice C uses $15$, not the product $8(7)$. Choice D matches $8x^2+56$. The answer is D.
